# Aspicio

> Aspicio (Latin: "I look at") is an open-source, TypeScript-first DXF
> engine: one framework-free parse → tessellate pipeline that serves
> people (an interactive WebGL viewer), applications (React components
> and a headless renderer), and AI agents (structured facts, PNG
> renders, and an interactive in-chat viewer over HTTP and MCP).

Aspicio is developed and maintained by [FrontSail AI](https://frontsail.ai/),
released under the MIT license.

## Why Aspicio

- One engine, every surface: the browser viewer, the web components
  and their React/Vue/Svelte bindings, the headless renderer, HTTP
  API, and MCP server are thin adapters over
  the same parse → tessellate core, so a drawing reads identically
  everywhere — no drift between what a person sees and what an agent
  is told.
- Built for AI agents, not retrofitted: `describe_dxf` returns
  structured JSON facts (units, bounds, layers with the colors actually
  drawn, entity counts, and the drawing's text content), so an agent
  reads a title block or a dimension value directly — no OCR, no
  vision round-trip.
- An interactive in-chat viewer via the open MCP Apps extension:
  `view_dxf` puts a pannable, zoomable, layer-toggling WebGL viewer
  directly in ChatGPT or Claude conversations. The widget is locked to
  the drawing the tool call delivered and makes no network requests.
- Broad entity coverage that never fails hard: lines, arcs, circles,
  ellipses, polylines with bulges, splines, TEXT/MTEXT, DIMENSION,
  SOLID/HATCH fills, nested INSERT blocks — anything unsupported is
  counted and reported, never fatal.
- Runs anywhere TypeScript runs: browser, Node, and Cloudflare
  Workers. WebGL rendering is batched to one draw call per layer, so
  large drawings stay interactive.
- Privacy-first hosted services: no accounts, no stored drawings, and
  guarded URL fetches (private-network blocking, size caps, redirect
  validation, timeouts). Everything is open source.

## Use it

- [Live demo](https://aspicio.frontsail.app/): drop in a DXF
  and explore it — layer panel, measuring, picking, export.
- [Hosted MCP server](https://aspicio-api.frontsail.app/mcp):
  remote MCP over Streamable HTTP — `describe_dxf`, `render_dxf`, and
  the `view_dxf` in-chat viewer. No install.
- Local MCP server: `npx -y @aspicio/mcp` — adds local-file support,
  reads drawings in-process, never uploads them.
- [HTTP API](https://aspicio-api.frontsail.app/openapi.json):
  `GET /describe?src=<dxf-url>` and
  `GET /render?src=<dxf-url>&format=png|svg`, self-described via
  OpenAPI 3.1.
- npm packages: `@aspicio/core` (viewer + headless pipeline),
  `@aspicio/elements` (framework-neutral web components:
  `<aspicio-embed>`, `<aspicio-preview>`, `<aspicio-layer-panel>`),
  and `@aspicio/react` / `@aspicio/vue` / `@aspicio/svelte` (the same
  three components — `<DxfEmbed>`, `<DxfPreview>`, `<DxfLayerPanel>` —
  as thin framework bindings over the elements).

## Docs

- [Repository](https://github.com/frontsail-ai/aspicio): source,
  issues, and contribution guide.
- [Architecture](https://github.com/frontsail-ai/aspicio/blob/master/docs/architecture.md):
  the pipeline and its layers.
- [Privacy policy](https://aspicio.frontsail.app/privacy/) ·
  [Terms of use](https://aspicio.frontsail.app/terms/)

contact [support@frontsail.ai](mailto:support@frontsail.ai).
